title: MA Higher Education Capstone Conference
description: The MA Higher Education program is hosting is annual Capstone Conference on Friday, May 1st 2026 from 5:30PM to 8:00PM in University Hall Room 1010. The Capstone Conference provides students in the program with an opportunity to share and present about the findings from their Capstone course, the final course in our program. Join us to celebrate our graduating students!

title: Visit the University Galleries' Spring 2026 Exhibition! Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts
location: George Segal Gallery, 4th Floor of the Red Hawk Deck
description:
On view from February 3 – May 3, 2026 in the George Segal Gallery. Public hours are Tuesday – Friday, 12 to 4 pm. 
   
 Admission is free, open to the public.  Learn more about this exhibition 
   
 Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts  is a group exhibition presented by the Office for Development and Alumni Engagement and hosted by the University Galleries.  Carpe Diem  brings together twelve accomplished alumni working in a range of media. 
   
 Curated by Sally Morgan Lehman, who has taught in the Department of Art and Design at Montclair and is owner and director of Morgan Lehman Gallery in New York City,  Carpe Diem  highlights the breadth of artistic practices fostered at Montclair and the lasting impact of its visual arts alumni. 
   
 Featured artists include Flávia Berindoague ’13, Bisa Butler ’05, Jonathan Calm ’97, Kyle Coniglio ’10, Alyssa Fanning ’12, Allen Ginsberg (1943), Márta Kucsora (2005–06), Michael Manning ’11, Nancy Bergman Pantirer ’80, Tiffany Perez ’09, William Pope.L ’78, and Maria Valdivia ’24.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC |  Opera Workshop Section 2: Woven
description:
Opera Workshop Section 2: "Woven" 
 An Opera Pastiche 
 Rebecca Jo Loeb,  director William Hobbs,  pianist Victoria Schwartzman,  pianist 
 Van Fossan Theatre Bloomfield College 
 FREE + OPEN TO THE PUBLIC | BLOOMFIELD COLLEGE OF MONTCLAIR STATE UNIVERSITY 449 Franklin St BLOOMFIELD, N O

title: Mindfulness Meditation
description:
Online. Mindfulness Meditation is the practice of being self-aware with an extra focus on your breath. Open to all who are interested in learning about meditation and developing mindfulness in daily life. Participate with video or audio only. 
 **Please note that we cannot verify attendance at this event

title: Red Hawk Pantry
description:
Visiting the Pantry: 
 1) Please have your Event Pass, CWID and have a  PantrySoft Registration .  2) Please bring your own shopping bag and ID card! ***Visitors are allowed to come once/week*** 
 Our Mission:   We aim to address food insecurity across Montclair State University. The Red Hawk Pantry is open to all students, staff, faculty, and Alumni at no cost. If you are unable to visit during our regular hours, please reach out via email at redhawkpantry@montclair.edu Stay updated by following us on Instagram: @montclair_redhawkpantry

 Wednesdays: 5-7pm 
 Saturdays: 11-1pm

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | University West African Drumming and Dance Ensemble
description:
Invite your friends, family, colleagues, students, and anyone else you like to our interactive performance on  MONDAY, May 4th, from 5-6pm . This will be a memorial concert in honor of  Emmanuel Agbeli . We’ll be playing  Slow and Fast Agbekor, Adzrowo, Sikyi  and  Kpanlogo. 
   
 It’s FREE and we’ll have refreshments to follow for everyone!

description:
Opera Workshop Section 1: Schemers, Dreamers, Lovers, & Leavers 
 Join us for a night where passion sings, ambition plots, and fate has the final note!

 Van Fossan Theatre Bloomfield College 
 FREE + OPEN TO THE PUBLIC | BLOOMFIELD COLLEGE OF MONTCLAIR STATE UNIVERSITY 449 Franklin St BLOOMFIELD, NJ

title: CPT Workshop - International Students (F-1 Visa Holders) Only
description: Learn more about Curricular Practical Training (CPT) for F-1 students. Any student planning to apply for CPT is REQUIRED to attend a workshop before they can apply. This workshop is for CURRENT students ONLY - any other attendees will be removed.

title: OPT Workshop - International Students (F-1 Visa Holders) Only
description: Learn more about pre-completion and post-completion Optional Practical Training (OPT) for F-1 students. Any student planning to apply for OPT is REQUIRED to attend a workshop before they can apply. This workshop is for CURRENT students ONLY - any other attendees will be removed.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Cali Pathways Project Spring Showcase 1
description:
Cali Pathways Project Spring Showcase 1 
 Cali Pathways students take the stage to share their growth, talent, and hard work through music. 
 The Cali School of Music at Montclair State University aims to create dynamic and comprehensive pathways to higher education and careers in music for under-resourced students. Our goal is to empower these young musicians to pursue their dreams and to make higher education more accessible.

title: Express Yourself! A Day of Creativity, Community and Discovery
organization: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS)
description:
Step into a world of creativity, movement and shared ideas at Express Yourself!, a vibrant day of arts, culture and creative exploration taking place on Saturday, May 9, in partnership with the Montclair Literary Festival. Open to students, faculty and the wider community, this celebration invites everyone to explore creative expression in many forms. 
 Join us for hands-on workshops and interactive experiences on  Saturday, May 9, 2026 . All events are open to all and free, but registration is required. Sign up for a single workshop or all six! 
 Register to Attend 
 Acting Out: Improv Group Style 9 -10:15 a.m., Life Hall room 1200 Crowd Source Acting is about using improvisation to act in the moment. Participants will be guided in an improvisational game that allows them to experience dropping into a roll and having the inspiration of those around them to achieve a common goal. That goal could be silly, serious, or absurd. Join us for a round or two of a game called Family Portraits, guided by Theatre department practitioners. 
 A Moving Experience For All 10:30-11:45 a.m., Life Hall 1210 This session will be a dance class for beginners, emphasizing freedom of movement and some basic dance moves. 
 Got Art? Take This Workshop 12:00-1:15 p.m., Calcia Hall 245 Participants will explore a visual arts technique such as collage, clay sculpting, or mixed media and create a unique work. Guided by an artist educator, attendees will learn simple methods and experiment with materials during the session. 
 Snap Snap: Taking Just the Right Picture 1:30-2:45 p.m., Feliciano School of Business Lawn This digital photography workshop will start with a presentation on the powerful choices available for capturing and documenting the world with your camera. Each participant will be given directions for taking a photograph from a specific location on campus. Jenelle Covino, Adjunct Professor of Art and Design, will guide participants to utilize basic photo retouching principles to bring each digital photograph to life. You can bring your digital camera or use your cell phone to photograph for this workshop. This workshop is suitable for all ages and all experience levels of photography. 
 Drumming Up a Crowd, the West African Way 3:00-4:15 p.m.; Feliciano School of Business Lawn The Montclair State University West African Drumming and Dance Ensemble will perform and engage everyone to participate in traditional West African recreational music. We play a strong weave of polyrhythms on traditional drums, bells and rattles, and those in attendance will drum with us, sing with us in call and response in a West African language, and dance traditional movements with us, directed by calls from the lead drum. 
 Let’s Get Physical: Writing Poetry about Memory and the Body 4:30-5:45 p.m. Feliciano School of Business Room 140 In this generative poetry workshop, we will begin by bringing our memories to life on the page. We will then expand to writing about embodied experiences. Participants will leave this workshop having written two new poems and having received feedback on one of the poems. They will also leave with a few additional tools in their writers’ toolbox.

title: FAFSA Completion Support Pop-Up Event
description: Do you need help completing your FAFSA? Stop by our FAFSA Support Pop-Up to get your questions answered and receive one-on-one guidance from Financial Aid Office staff. Whether you’re just getting started or need help finishing your application, we’re here to make the process easier and help you stay on track for financial aid.

title: Library 101
description:
This workshop is your introduction to all the ways Montclair State University Libraries support your academic success. It covers what resources are available to you (e.g., books, articles, films, databases) and how you can access them, including through interlibrary loan. You'll learn how to navigate the library website, distinguish between popular and scholarly sources, and get help from a librarian when working on a research assignment. 
 Register Here:  https://montclair.libcal.com/event/16797394

description: This event is EXCLUSIVE to National Leadership Conference (NLC) qualifiers. Students with club membership CANNOT attend. Congratulations on qualifying for the NLC! Join us for our first National conference event as students represent Montclair State University on the National stage.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond. 
 All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

title: Study Abroad Fair Fall 2026
description:
Ready to take your education global? Join us at the Study Abroad Fair and explore a world of opportunities waiting just beyond your campus. Discover programs around the world, meet representatives from our partners and providers, and hear firsthand stories from students who’ve already taken the leap. 
 Whether you're dreaming of practicing your language skills, expanding your horizons in a new corner of the globe, or even seeing a different part of the United States, this is your chance to find the perfect fit. Learn about scholarships, internships, and how to make your journey abroad both affordable and unforgettable. 
 📅 Date: Wednesday, September 9, 2026 📍 Location: Student Center Ballrooms ⏰ Time: 1:30-3:30pm

title: Fall 2026 Part-Time Job Fair
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
description:
The Fall Part-time Job Fair offers students the opportunity to meet with both on-campus and off-campus employers interested in hiring various positions. The Fair will be held in the Student Center Ballrooms from 1-3 p.m., on Wednesday, September 16.  
 
 More Information : 
 
 Bring plenty of copies of your resume 
 Students must bring their Montclair State University Student IDs 
 Registration is required through Handshake 
 The location is the Student Center Ballrooms September 16, 2016 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM

title: Fall 2026 Career And Internship Fair
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
description:
Looking to launch your career and gain experience through a full-time job or internship? The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections invites students of all levels to network with employers in a wide variety of industries! 
 More Information : 
 
 Open to all students, alumni, and community members 
 Bring plenty of copies of your resume 
 Students must bring their Montclair State University Student IDs 
 Registration is required through Handshake. 
 
 October 7 1:00 PM -  4:00 PM 
 UNIVERSITY HALL CONFERENCE CENTER

organization: College of Science and Mathematics (CSAM) Career Services
description:
Workshop
